query: Humane AI Pin residue inventory sale leftover units fate after HP deal 2025

mako.raw:
{"query":"Humane AI Pin residue inventory sale leftover units fate after HP deal 2025","model":"mako","response_time_ms":953,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://complexdiscovery.com/the-end-of-humane-ai-pin-hps-strategic-shift-toward-ai-integration/","title":"The End of Humane AI Pin: HP’s Strategic Shift Toward AI Integration","content":"As HP reshapes Humane’s assets into a more cohesive AI strategy, this development underscores the importance of aligning cutting-edge technology with practical, user-focused applications.
The Humane AI Pin, a wearable device created by ex-Apple designers Imran Chaudhri and Bethany Bongiorno, will permanently shut down its functionality by February 28, 2025, at 12 PM PT.
Once marketed as a groundbreaking innovation in personal AI, the $700 wearable will lose all features connected to Humane’s servers, including calling, messaging, AI queries, and cloud access.
The shutdown follows HP’s acquisition of Humane for $116 million—a sharp drop from previous valuations that was rumored to reach as high as $1 billion.
A critical review by tech influencer Marques Brownlee, viewed over 8.5 million times, highlighted persistent performance issues, laggy responses, and underwhelming functionality—factors that ultimately led to widespread customer disappointment.
By mid-2024, sales had slowed dramatically, and return rates began to surpass new purchases.
Users are advised to download any personal data before the deadline.
Refunds are being offered only to customers who purchased the device after November 15, 2024, with a request deadline of February 27, leaving many early adopters frustrated by the narrow refund policy.
The fall of the Humane AI Pin offers valuable lessons for tech startups and investors.
With an estimated 10,000 unsold or returned units contributing to global e-waste, environmental experts have pointed to the device’s glued-in battery design as a barrier to effective recycling."}]}

Humane AI Pin customers have until Feb. 27 to get a refund, while HP plans to stick AI in its printers as it acquires Humane for $116 million. Humane founders,  Imran Chaudhri and Bethany Bongiorno, are both heading up a new division within HP. Humane fell down the drain of AI hype, and legacy tech company HP is here to scrape up whatever is left over of the AI wearable’s once-ambitions. HP bought up Humane’s CosmOS operating system and IP, including the tech for the AI Pin, for $116 million. Tuan Tran, HP’s former president of the company’s printing arm and new lead on the company’s AI endeavors, said in a release that the buyout would “rapidly accelerate our ability to develop a new generation of devices that seamlessly orchestrate AI requests both locally and in the cloud.” That means HP is looking to develop its own suite of AI-centric devices.","score":0.53619206,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.84,"request_id":"521fb880-d5e7-447a-bad3-0725f6a68e73"}
